fault_hypothesis: The comparison on line 67 uses `>` instead of `<`. With `bucket > rate`, a rate of 0 never samples (bucket is always > 0) and a rate of 1 always samples only when bucket > 1 (never), which is backwards. The correct expression should be `bucket < rate` so that higher rates include more buckets.

spec.expr: (rate == 0 and not (bucket__1 > rate)) or (rate == 1 and (bucket__1 > rate)) or (rate > 0 and rate < 1)

constraints.intent: At rate=0 the result must be False (no item sampled) and at rate=1 it must be True (all sampled); bucket>rate violates both boundary conditions.
The computed bucket must be in [0,1] so that comparison with rate is meaningful for boundary rates 0 and 1.
If rate==1, every bucket in [0,1) should be sampled; bucket<rate would be True for all valid buckets, but bucket>rate would be False for all, exposing the wrong operator.
When rate is 0, the function must never return True (no log line should be sampled).
